segments.s546-g81: دگر introduces a different class (‘others’). آنک ... که‌اند is a relative construction: آنک = ‘those who/that which’ and که‌اند = ‘that are/ who are’, so the phrase reads ‘others, those who are wise in the city’ (در شهر دانا = ‘wise in the city’ with the locative در preceding شهر). Word order is topical (دگر) then the relative clause.
segments.s546-g82: گر here is concessive ‘though/even if’. از is causal ‘because of / on account of’, linking نیستی (‘nonexistence’) as the cause; ناتوانا is a predicative adjective ‘powerless/helpless’. The final که‌اند ties this adjectival description back into the relative clause started by آنک (i.e. ‘who are powerless because of nonexistence’).
segments.s546-g83: دگر کیست = ‘who else is there?’ (دگر = ‘other/else’, کی = ‘who’, ست = copula ‘is’). آنک introduces the relative clause ‘who...’; از در here literally ‘from the door/gate’ is an idiom for ‘of the royal court / at the king’s presence’ (در used as ‘gate/court’), and پادشاست indicates relation to the king (here ‘of the king/court’).
segments.s546-g84: جهاندیده is an adjective ‘worldly‑wise / experienced in the world’. پیرست is written as one word but functions as پیر + است ‘is old/elder’ (here describing the same person). و‌گر = ‘or if not / or else’ introduces an alternative; پارساست = ‘is pious’. Overall: ‘...worldly‑wise and old, or else pious.’
segments.s546-g85: شهنشاه گوید is simple present narration ‘the shah says’. که introduces the content clause. از رنج من = ‘because of / on account of my suffering’ (از marking the cause and من = ‘my’).
segments.s546-g86: مبادا is a jussive/negative wish ‘may it not be / let there not be’, so مبادا کسی شاد = ‘may no one be glad’. بی‌گنج من = ‘without my treasure / at my being without wealth’ (بی‌- = ‘without’, گنج = ‘treasure’, من = ‘my’), completing the shah’s wish that no one rejoice at his suffering or his lack of wealth.
segments.s546-g87: مگر here introduces a rhetorical question meaning roughly ‘is there not / except’ (used colloquially as ‘is there anyone...’). مرد با دانش و یادگیر = ‘a man with knowledge and a learner’ (با marks accompaniment/possession: ‘with knowledge’; یادگیر = ‘one who learns / a learner’).
segments.s546-g88: چه is an exclamative/interrogative ‘how/what’ introducing the evaluative clause; نیکوتر is the comparative ‘better’ (from نیکو). از is the comparative marker here (‘than’). مرد دانا و پیر = ‘a wise and old man’ (دانا = wise, پیر = old), so the whole clause asks how much better such a (learning) man is than a wise, elderly man.
segments.s546-g1: کنون is an adverb placed up front meaning 'now'. از here introduces the topic or theme ('about/concerning'), so "از خردمندی اردشیر" = 'about Ardashir's wisdom' — خردمندی is a nominal compound 'wisdom/prudence' and اردشیر follows it as the possessor/name (no ezāfe in the written text, but conceptually 'خردمندیِ اردشیر').
segments.s546-g2: سخن functions as the direct object/topic 'the tale/word'. بشنو is a second-person imperative (or jussive) 'listen/hear'. و links the two imperatives. به in the idiom 'یک به یک' is the connective often translated 'by/one by one' and یادگیر here is an imperative verb 'learn!' (classical form), not the nominal 'learner'.
segments.s546-g3: بکوشید is the polite/imperative or exhortative plural of کوشیدن 'strive/try'. و simply connects clauses. آیین نیکو is a noun phrase 'a good/custom/practice' and نهاد here is a past/causative verbal form 'placed/established' (i.e., 'set up' or 'establish').
segments.s546-g4: بگسترد is a subjunctive/jussive-form verb meaning 'let it be spread' or an imperative sense 'spread'. بر هر سوی = 'over/throughout every direction' (بر + هر + سوی). مهر و داد are coordinated nouns meaning 'kindness/affection' and 'justice' respectively.
segments.s546-g5: به درگاه = 'to the (royal) court/presence' (به + درگاه). چون introduces the temporal clause 'when' (classical usage), and خواست is the past form 'he wished/desired' (here governing the following action). لشکر فزون is 'a large/abundant army' (modifier فزون after the noun).
segments.s546-g6: فرستاد is the simple past 'he sent/dispatches'. بر هر سوی = 'to/toward every direction' (بر as 'toward/over' + هر سوی). رهنمون here means 'guide/guidance' (object of فرستاد, understood as 'he sent guides' or 'sent guidance').
segments.s546-g7: که تا together mark a purposive/result clause 'so that' or 'in order that'. هرکسی را که دارد پسر literally 'whoever has a son' — را is the definite object/topic marker placed here for emphasis/clarity, که is the relative 'who/that', دارد is the present tense 'has/possesses', and پسر is 'son'.
segments.s546-g8: نماند is negated or desiderative 'let there not remain / there will not be left'. The following که introduces what will not remain: بالا کند is a verbal phrase where بالا 'high/up' combines with کند (present/subjunctive of 'to do') meaning 'to raise/promote' or 'make high'. بی‌هنر is an adjective 'unskilled'. Altogether: 'may there not remain that an unskilled one be raised' — i.e., nobody's son should remain neglected/untrained.
segments.s546-g257: چو introduces a conditional ('if') and is followed by خواهی, 2nd-person present 'you want/wish'. که is the complementizer introducing the subordinate clause; آزاد باشی is the subjunctive/optative clause 'that you be free' (باشی is the subjunctive form 'you be'). ز is the poetic preposition 'from', and رنج means 'suffering' or 'pain'. The whole segment reads literally 'If you want that you be free from suffering.'
segments.s546-g258: This is a verbless descriptive/advice phrase that continues the idea of باشی: the verb 'be' from the previous clause is understood to govern these predicates. بی‌آزار and بی‌رنج are adjectival/nominal compounds meaning 'harmless' and 'untroubled' (بی- = without). آگنده is an adjective 'filled/full (of)', modifying گنج 'treasure' — together 'full of treasure'.
segments.s546-g259: بی‌آزاری here is a nominalized adjective meaning 'harmlessness' or 'not harming'; زیردستان ('your') subjects/vassals follows as the affected parties. گزین is the imperative 'choose' (second-person singular). Word order is object/quality + beneficiary + imperative: 'Choose harmlessness toward your subjects.'
segments.s546-g260: بیابی is a present/future sense 'you will find/you will receive' (second-person). ز هرکس means 'from everyone' (i.e. on the part of each person). به introduces the beneficiary/relation 'for' or 'in regard to'; داد is 'justice', and آفرین is a noun 'praise/acclamation' (not the verbal stem). Together: 'you will receive from everyone praise for (your) justice.'
segments.s546-g193: A terse, poetic list introduced by the preposition ز (an archaic/poetic form of از, 'from' or 'concerning'). The phrase strings together paired items with و 'and'; وز here is the compressed sequence و + ز ('and from'). Word order is nominal: preposition + noun, and the whole segment functions as the first part of an enumerative series.
segments.s546-g194: Continuation of the same enumerative structure: ز introduces another item ('concerning'), آیین is a noun meaning custom/practice, and the repeated وز (here again representing و + ز) links further items. لشکرش is a compound with possessive suffix -ش 'his'. The whole segment completes the list of topics.
segments.s546-g195: A past-tense verbal clause: به (to) + ایوانش 'his iwan/porch' as the goal, followed by بردی, the 2nd-person singular past of بردن 'you took/brought'. فرستاده‌وار is a predicative/adverbial compound meaning 'messenger-like' or 'as if an envoy' and modifies the manner of بردی.
segments.s546-g196: An independent clause with an older/poetic verb form: بیاراستی functions as a perfective/preterite verb 'you arranged/you equipped' (here used archaically), هرچ 'whatever', بودی 'was/were' (past or stative), and به کار 'for the work/for use' — together meaning 'you provided whatever was needed for the task.' Word order is verb + object + qualifying phrase.
segments.s546-g197: A temporal/sequence marker وزان پس ('from/and then after that') opens the clause; به + خوان 'to/for a banquet' names what was summoned, و links میش 'lamb/sheep (poetic, i.e. the roast)', and خواندی is the past tense verb here meaning 'you called/summoned'. The clause frames a summons to a feast.
segments.s546-g198: Simple verbal clause: بر تخت 'upon the throne' + زرینش 'his golden (throne)' with the possessive suffix -ش, and بنشاندی an archaic 2nd-person past causative/active 'you caused (him) to sit' or 'you seated (him)'. Word order is prepositional locative phrase followed by the verb of placement.
segments.s546-g199: A brief clause where به + نخچیر names the object 'to/with the spoil or game', and بردیش is an outcome/possession verb (poetic past form) meaning taking or gaining; با خویشتن 'with yourself' uses خویشتن emphatically to indicate accompaniment. The phrase describes carrying off spoils or taking game along.
segments.s546-g200: Nominal predication with شدی (past of شدن) 'became/was' followed by a noun phrase لشکر بیشمار انجمن 'an innumerable army (assembled/at the assembly)'. The adjectives follow the head noun (لشکر بیشمار) and انجمن names the gathering; overall: 'a countless army came together/was assembled.'
segments.s546-g89: "جهاندیدگان" is a plural noun meaning 'the worldly-wise/experienced people'; the postpositional particle "را" here marks the object/theme of desire; "همه" ('all/everyone') indicates that everyone among them is involved; "خواستار" is an adjectival/predicative word meaning 'desirous' or 'seeking' and together the sequence conveys 'all the worldly-wise are desirous (of)...'. Word order is poetic: the thing desired follows and is supplied by the next segment.
segments.s546-g90: A string of coordinated adjectives describing the desired person: "جوان" (young), "پسندیده" (pleasing/approved), and "بردبار" (patient/tolerant). The head noun is omitted (understood as 'one' or 'person'), so these adjectives function as the complement to "خواستار" from the previous segment.
segments.s546-g91: A plural noun phrase: "جوانان" (young men) modified by two adjectival/participial attributes: "دانا" (wise) and "دانش‌پذیر" (literally 'knowledge-accepting', i.e. teachable). The second adjective is a compound describing receptivity to learning rather than the noun 'student'.
segments.s546-g92: "سزد‌گر" is a modal/qualifying word meaning 'appropriate' or 'fitting' (it gives the sense of what ought to be done); "نشینند" is 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive/optative 'they should sit' (poetic use of the form to express recommendation); "بر جای پیر" is a prepositional phrase 'on/upon the place of the elder' meaning 'in the elder's seat/position'. Together: 'it is fitting that they sit in the elder's place.'
segments.s546-g93: "چو" here means 'when' introducing a temporal clause; "لشکرش" is 'his army' (لشکر + possessive ـش); "رفتی" is the 2nd-person past form 'you went' used in narrative/imperative-patterns to mean 'when (you/it) go'; "به جایی به جنگ" literally 'to some place to battle' = 'out somewhere to fight'. The clause sets the time/situation for the advice that follows.
segments.s546-g94: "خرد یار کردی" literally 'make wisdom (your) companion' where "خرد" is 'wisdom', "یار" is 'companion/ally', and "کردی" is 2nd-person past 'you made' used here as an instructive counsel ('make X your companion'). The following coordinated nouns "و رای و درنگ" add 'and counsel and deliberation/pausing' as further companions — i.e., take counsel and deliberate as you go to battle.
segments.s546-g95: "فرستاده‌ای" is used as 'a messenger/one sent' (here a noun phrase, formed from the participle), "برگزیدی" is 2nd-person past 'you chose/appointed', and "دبیر" ('secretary/scribe') is in apposition specifying that messenger. Poetic word order places the noun phrase before the verb and the apposition after it.
segments.s546-g96: A sequence of attributes describing that secretary: "خردمند" (wise), then the prepositional attribute "با دانش" ('with knowledge' = learned), and finally "یادگیر" ('learner' or 'quick to learn'), which here means someone who readily acquires or memorizes knowledge. These modifiers are cumulative apposition to "دبیر" in the previous segment.
segments.s546-g161: هرآنگه که ... شوی is a temporal conditional clause: هرآنگه (an archaic/formal adverb) means 'whenever'; که is the relative/subordinating particle 'that/when'; از دشمن is a prepositional phrase 'from the enemy'; ایمن is an adjectival predicate 'safe/protected (from)'; شوی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive form of شدن used here to mean 'you become/are' and links the subordinate temporal clause to the main clause that follows.
segments.s546-g162: This is the main clause: سخن گفتن is a noun+infinitive construction 'speaking / speech (to speak)'; کس is an indefinite 'anyone' (here in the scope of negation); همی is an archaic continuous/affirmative particle often marking ongoing or habitual action; نشنوی is 2nd-person present negative 'you will not hear' (from شنیدن). Word order is typical: object (سخن گفتن کس) then verb (نشنوی).
segments.s546-g163: غنیمت بدان بخش is an imperative/imperative-like verbless instruction 'count/consider that portion as booty' where غنیمت is the noun 'booty/prize', ب (here the preposition/prefix) + دان make بدان 'consider it' (دان used in the sense 'to consider'), and بخش 'portion/part'. کو جنگ جست is a relative phrase 'which rushed into battle' — کو functions as a poetic relative ('which/that') and جنگ جست is a compact past action 'sprang/charged into battle' (جست here = 'sprang/rushed').
segments.s546-g164: This clause explains the cost: به مردی دل 'for a man, his heart' (به marks beneficiary/target), از جان شیرین 'from his sweet life' (از = 'from/of'), and بشست is the past form meaning literally 'washed' (here metaphorical: his heart was washed away/left his dear life). The verb is past (third-person singular) and the clause is descriptive of the result of joining battle.
segments.s546-g165: هرانکس که ... اسیر is a protasis 'whoever ... is taken captive': هرانکس 'whoever', که the relative particle, گردد is a subjunctive/presentive form of گشتن 'become' (here 'becomes'), به دستت is a prepositional phrase 'into your hand' where -ت is the 2nd-person possessive suffix attached to دست, and اسیر is the predicate noun 'a captive'. Word order places the relative clause before the main command.
segments.s546-g166: بدین بارگاه آورش ناگزیر is an imperative instruction: بدین is the preposition ب 'to/into' + این ('this' in classical form, here written/segmented as دین) giving 'to this'; بارگاه 'the royal court'; آورش is the archaic/suffixal object-marked form of آوردن 'bring him' (آور + -ش 'him'); ناگزیر is an adjective/adverb 'inevitable/compelled' used here to mean 'without fail / you must (compel) bring him.'
segments.s546-g167: من از بهر ایشان یکی شارستان is the subject and object: من 'I'; از بهر ایشان is an idiomatic prepositional phrase 'for their sake/for them' (از بهر = 'for'); یکی شارستان 'a certain/a single town' (یکی = 'one/a certain', شارستان = 'city/town'). The sequence places the purpose phrase before the direct object 'a city'.
segments.s546-g168: برآرم is a 1st-person present/future-stative of برآوردن 'I will raise/build/erect'; به بومی here indicates location or ground 'on/at a tract (بوم)'; که introduces a relative clause 'that/which'; بد خارستان is the relative predicate 'a bad/ill (بد) thorny thicket (خارستان)'. Overall: 'I will raise a town on local ground that is a bad thorny waste.'
segments.s546-g17: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. جنگ آمدی is a poetic past clause 'war came' (آمدی here functions as the past arrival of the event). نورسیده جوان is a noun phrase: نورسیده (literally 'newly-born/untested') modifies جوان ('young/young man'), giving 'an inexperienced young man'. Word order is conjunction + subject-clause + adjectival phrase.
segments.s546-g18: برفتی is a past action 'went forth' (poetic past form). ز here means 'from' (poetic preposition). درگاه denotes the palace threshold or royal court. با is the preposition 'with' linking پهلوان ('champion, hero') as the companion. The whole segment says that he went out of the court in company with the champion.
segments.s546-g19: یکی here means 'a certain' (introducing an individual). موبدان is the plural/collective 'mōbed' (Zoroastrian priest); را is the definite/object marker placed after موبدان for emphasis/definiteness. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of' introducing the qualifier کارآگهان ('knowledgeable about affairs'), so the phrase names 'a certain priest who was knowledgeable in affairs.'
segments.s546-g20: که is the relative pronoun 'who/that' introducing a clause describing the mōbed. بودی is the past copula 'was'. خریدار here is used idiomatically as 'one who takes on or accepts' (literally 'buyer'), followed by کار جهان 'the work/affairs of the world' as its object; together they mean he undertook or took charge of worldly tasks.
segments.s546-g21: ابر here is an archaic/poetic element meaning roughly 'out of' or 'among' (not the literal 'cloud'); هر هزاری is 'of every thousand' (هر = every, هزاری = thousand-unit), and یکی کارجوی means 'one job-seeker' or 'one who sought work.' The phrase gives a proportion: one applicant per thousand.
segments.s546-g22: برفتی again is a past verb 'sent/let go' or 'went forth' in narrative. نگه داشتی is a compound where نگه ('watch/keeping') combines with داشتن ('to have/keep') to mean 'kept watch' or 'kept under observation'. کار اوی is 'his work' (اوی is the archaic possessive pronoun 'his/its'); the clause says that he sent the one forth and kept an eye on his task.
segments.s546-g23: هرانکس 'whosoever' begins the conditional/relative idea; که introduces the clause 'who, when in battle...'. در جنگ is the locative 'in battle', and سست is the adjective 'weak' or 'feeble'. آمدی is the past arrival 'came' — together: 'whoever came into battle weak'.
segments.s546-g24: This is a terse poetic result clause. به آورد here is causative/performative 'brought (about or back)', ناتن‌درست is the adjective 'unsound / physically unfit', and آمدی repeats the past 'came/returned' — the effect is that the weak entrant brought about/returned in an unsound or faulty condition. The syntax is compressed and poetic: verb-object-verb sequence expressing outcome.
segments.s546-g177: This phrase groups a noun phrase plus a participle: بره (a lamb, here used metaphorically = 'meek/innocent') is the patient noun; بر + بدی خان is a prepositional phrase 'before/against the evil khan' (بر = toward/in the presence of); او is the object pronoun 'him'; ساخته is the perfect participle/past form of ساختن ('made, fashioned') serving as the main verbal element — together meaning 'he made him a lamb before the evil khan' (poetic word order places the object pronoun after the noun phrase).
segments.s546-g178: کنارنگ is an adverbial/adjunct meaning 'aside, apart' or 'to one side'; زان = 'from that' (referring back to the preceding matter); کار = 'deed/affair'; پرداخته is the past participle of پرداختن ('to attend to / deal with'), here used to indicate the action on that affair. The sequence reads 'and, being apart/from that, he dealt with (or ceased to deal with) that affair' — in context it conveys withdrawal from or cessation of engagement with that business.
segments.s546-g179: ز here is the poetic/prepositional 'from/of'; پوشیدنیها is the plural noun 'garments, clothes' (things to wear); و = 'and'; از = 'from/of' introducing the second item in the list; خوردنی = 'edibles/food'. The phrase lists material needs: 'of clothes and of food'.
segments.s546-g180: نیازش = نیاز + ـش ('his/its need') meaning 'he had need'; نبودی is the past negative copula 'was not' (here 'had not / there was not for him'); به = a preposition 'for/to' marking purpose; گستردنی is a deverbal adjective/masdar-like form from گستردن 'to spread/lay out' — 'for spreading' (i.e. for any display/banquet). Together: 'he had no need of clothes or food for any spreading/show.'
segments.s546-g181: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; آگه 'aware' (adjective) plus شدی (past of شدن 'to become') forms 'became aware' — a past-tense clause 'when he learned'; زان = 'of/from that' referring to the previously mentioned report; سخن = 'speech/news/word'; کاردار = the agent 'chargé d'affaires' or 'envoy' who is the subject. Word order places the temporal clause before the main indirect-question clause.
segments.s546-g182: که introduces the content of what was learned (an indirect question/clause 'about which ...'); او = 'he'; بر چه آمد is an idiomatic question 'for what purpose did he come' (بر before چه gives the sense 'to/for what'); آمد is the past tense 'came'; بر شهریار = 'to the king' with بر used as the preposition 'to/toward' plus شهریار 'the king'. The clause thus means 'about why he had come to the king.'
segments.s546-g183: This is a coordinated nominal group: هیونی (here functioning as a proper name or epithet) followed by the postpositive adjective سرافراز ('lofty/triumphant'); و = 'and' links the next coordinated noun phrase; مردی is an indefinite noun 'a man' (emphatic/characteristic), and دبیر in apposition specifies his role 'a scribe/secretary'. The construction lists two persons/epithets: 'Hiyuni the lofty, and a man who was a scribe.'
segments.s546-g184: برفتی is an archaic/poetic past-form of رفتن ('he went') used here as the narrative past 'went/rode'; به نزدیک = an idiom 'to the presence of / near to' (به + نزدیک); شاه اردشیر = 'King Ardashir' (شاه = title 'king', اردشیر = proper name). The whole segment means '(they) went to the king Ardashir's presence.'
segments.s546-g113: This is a temporal introductory phrase plus the subject and locative: 'وزان پس' (= 'from that time / after that') sets the sequence; 'یکی مرد' is an indefinite subject 'a man' (note word order: adjective/quantifier before noun); 'بر پشت پیل' is a prepositional phrase 'upon the elephant's back' with preposition بر + noun phrase پشت پیل.
segments.s546-g114: This clause gives the action and its consequence. 'نشستی' is a past action 'sat' (poetic/archaic past of نشستن) introducing what the man did; 'که' here is the relative/conjunction 'when/that'; 'رفتی' (went/moved) describes the elephant's motion; 'خروشش' is the elephant's roar (possessive suffix); 'دو میل' expresses extent/distance. The verbs are in past narrative form and the relative 'که' links the motion to the roar.
segments.s546-g115: 'زدی بانگ' is a verb + object phrase where the verb زدی (past of زدن) here means 'uttered/sounded' and بانگ is 'a cry/shout'; 'کای' is the vocative particle 'O' addressing the addressee; 'نامداران جنگ' is the noun phrase 'renowned ones/men of war' (name–bearers = famous fighters). Word order is Verb–Object then the vocative call and its apposition.
segments.s546-g116: This is a relative clause headed by 'هرانکس که' ('whoever, whosoever') followed by the verb 'دارد' ('has/possesses') and a list of objects 'دل و نام و ننگ'. 'دارد' is present/persistent possession used in narrative to indicate qualification; the conjunctions 'و' simply join the nouns.
segments.s546-g117: 'نباید' is an impersonal modal/obligation marker 'it should not / ought not'; 'که' introduces the subordinate clause; 'بر هیچ درویش رنج' is the prepositional object 'upon no/none of the poor (druvish) should suffering [fall]'. The phrase expresses a general prohibition/expectation that no poor person be afflicted.
segments.s546-g118: This is a conditional concessive clause. 'رسد‌گر' is the archaic conditional sequence 'رسد گر' (= 'if it happens / if it should be that'); 'بر آنکس' = 'upon that person/for that one'; 'بود' is past-tense 'was' used here in the conditional sense ('if that one had'); 'نام و گنج' are the items ('fame and treasure') that follow. The whole clause concedes the case even if he possessed fame and treasure.
segments.s546-g119: 'به هر منزلی' = 'at/to every house/dwelling/station' (preposition + quantifier + place); 'در خورید و دهید' contains two parallel verbs: 'خورید' (eat / partake — here an exhortation or prescriptive present/imperative) and 'دهید' (give — imperative). The coordination 'و' links the two commands/actions appropriate to each stop/house.
segments.s546-g120: 'بران' is an archaic pronominal/prepositional element meaning 'to/on them' or 'thereupon'; 'زیردستان' is the plural noun 'subordinates/subjects'; 'سپاسی' is the noun 'thanks/gratitude'; 'نهید' is the verb form 'place/put/bestow' (2nd‑person plural or jussive/imperative in poetic register). Together the phrase issues the command to bestow thanks/praise upon the subordinates.
segments.s546-g33: چنین (thus) + تا (until) introduces the extent to which the action continues. سپاهش is سپاه + the enclitic -ش (‘his army’); it appears before the verb as the subject/topic. رسید is a simple past (3rd person singular) ‘arrived/reached’ and completes the clause; بدانجا is an adverbial destination ‘to that place’.
segments.s546-g34: که introduces a relative clause ('where/that') modifying 'بدانجا'. پهنای ایشان is a genitive-like phrase 'the breadth/extent of them' (their wide ranks) functioning as the subject. ستاره is the direct object placed before the verb (typical poetic order), and ندید is the negative past form 'did not see' (ن- prefix + دید).
segments.s546-g35: از ایشان is a prepositional phrase 'from/out of them'. کسی را uses the definite/object marker را after 'someone', making that referent specific or topical. که begins a relative clause describing that someone; بد modifies رای‌زن to mean 'a bad counsellor' (رای‌زن = one who gives counsel).
segments.s546-g36: برافراختندی is past tense, 3rd person plural 'they raised/erected'. سرش is سر + enclitic -ش 'his head' (object). ز introduces the source 'from' and انجمن is the noun 'assembly/meeting'. The clause literally says 'they raised his head from the assembly' (i.e. exalted or made him stand out in the council).
segments.s546-g37: The initial که + هرکس که is a prototypical double relative/conditional 'whoever'. خشنودی شاه is a genitive phrase 'the king's favor/approval' serving as the object, and جست is the past of جستن here meaning 'sought'. Word order places the object before the verb, common in classical/poetic Persian.
segments.s546-g38: زمین را is the definite object 'the land' (marked by را). به introduces an instrumental/associative phrase 'by/with' (به خوان دلیران = 'with/by the feast/table of the brave'). دلیران is the plural 'brave ones', and بشست is past 'he washed'—a metaphorical verb here meaning he 'covered/celebrated/filled the land' by that feast. The clause follows object + prepositional phrase + verb order.
segments.s546-g39: بیابد is a present/future-oriented form of یافتن (to obtain/receive) used here as 'he will obtain' or 'he shall receive' (3rd singular). ز من 'from me' marks the source, and خلعت شهریار is a possessive/construct meaning 'the robe of the king' (خلعت = robe of honor; شهریار = king), the direct object of بیابد.
segments.s546-g40: بود is the copula 'was/will be' linking the predicate; در جهان 'in the world' is an adverbial locative; نام او 'his name' is the subject (name/reputation) and یادگار is the predicative noun 'a memorial/memento'. The clause follows a classical inverted order: copula + locative + subject + predicate.
segments.s546-g153: چو is a temporal/subordinating conjunction here meaning "when" (introducing a condition). پیروز is an adjective "victorious" modifying the understood subject "you." گردی is the verb form meaning "you become" but its lemma senses in the provided list don't match this verbal use (it's a present/subjunctive/indicative form). ز کس is a short prepositional phrase "from/of anyone" (ز = "from/of" + کس = "anyone"). خون مریز is a negative imperative: خون ("blood") + مریز (negative imperative "do not spill"). The clause therefore orders the addressee not to spill blood, conditioned by the preceding temporal phrase.
segments.s546-g154: که introduces a causal/explanatory clause here ("for/because"). شد is the past of "to become" ("became"). دشمن is the subject "the enemy." بدکنش is a descriptive compound meaning someone who does evil/acts ill (an ill‑doer or becomes vicious); it functions adjectivally/nominatively of the enemy. در گریز is a locative/adverbial phrase "in flight/retreat." Together the clause gives the reason: the enemy becomes worse when in flight.
segments.s546-g155: چو here functions as a conditional "if." خواهد is the present of خواستن "wants/desires." ز دشمن کسی is an idiomatic phrase "from the enemy someone" = "anyone among the enemy." زینهار is a noun "a warning/entreaty." The whole clause means "if anyone of the enemy wishes (to give) a warning..."
segments.s546-g156: تو is the vocative/subject "you." زنهارده (a verbal/adjectival form) means "warned" or "on your guard" (a participial/adjectival use: be in the state of having been warned). باش is the imperative of بودن "be." و joins the two imperatives. کینه مدار is a negative command: کینه ("grudge/hatred") + مدار here functions as a prohibitive verb (in classical/poetic Persian it means "do not hold/keep"); the given lemma senses for مدار do not reflect this verbal use, so its sense is irregular here. The segment thus commands: "be warned and do not keep a grudge."
segments.s546-g157: چو is a temporal conjunction "when/if." تو is the subject "you." پشت دشمن is a noun phrase "the enemy's back/rear" (پشت = "back/rear" + دشمن = "enemy"). ببینی is the present/subjunctive/indicative form of دیدن "you see." به چیز is a short object/phrase "to/for a thing" used idiomatically with مپرداز to mean "about anything" (i.e., "do not bother about anything").
segments.s546-g158: مپرداز is a negative imperative formed from پرداختن (here "do not concern/pay attention/give thought"). و links the two prohibitions. مگذر is another negative imperative from گذشتن "do not pass/leave." هم adds the sense "also/even." از جای is the prepositional phrase "from your place/post." نیز reinforces "also/either." Together: "do not pay attention to anything, and do not also leave your place."
segments.s546-g159: نباید is an impersonal modal negative "one must not/it should not be that"; it introduces a prohibitive/general warning. که introduces the subordinate clause. ایمن شوید is the verb phrase "you become safe/secure" (ایمن = "safe" + شوید = 2nd-person plural/formal of شدن). از کمین is a prepositional phrase "from ambush/ambuscade." The clause therefore cautions against assuming safety from ambush.
segments.s546-g160: سپه is the noun "host/army." باشد is the subjunctive/existential form of بودن here (rendered "may be/is possible"). اندر is a poetic/prepositional form meaning "in/within." در in this collocation means "gate/door" (اندر در = "within the gate"). و links to another locative phrase. دشت is "plain." کین is an attributive noun meaning "hatred/vengeance," modifying the location ("plain of vengeance/hatred"). The clause warns that a host may be concealed at the gate and on the vengeful plain.
segments.s546-g73: و‌گر is the conditional 'if/but if' introducing a hypothetical; هیچ here functions as 'any/ever' (i.e. 'if any'), followed by the noun درویش 'dervish/ascetic' as the subject. خسپد is an archaic/poetic verb meaning 'become pale/turn white' (here from fear) and is followed by the prepositional phrase به بیم 'with fear'. Word order is conditional marker → subject → verb → prepositional circumstance.
segments.s546-g74: همی is an emphatic/adverbial particle (often ‘still/ever’ or marking a regular/habitual action). جان فروشی here is a nominalized verbal phrase meaning 'selling one's life' (جان = 'life'; فروشی 'selling' — an instance of a noun/participial form used to mean 'would sell' or 'sell'). The prepositional phrases به زر و به سیم 'for gold and for silver' state the price. Overall this clause expresses the (would) action that follows the conditional introduced earlier.
segments.s546-g75: هرانکس که begins a relative/generic clause 'whoever'. رفتی is a classical form of the verb 'to go' used generically here ('goes' / 'comes') and is followed by the prepositional phrase به درگاه شاه 'to the king's court/presence'. Note the relative clause syntax: 'whoever' + verb + destination.
segments.s546-g76: به شایسته کاری is a prepositional phrase meaning 'with/for a worthy deed' (شایسته = 'worthy', کاری here functions as 'deed/action' in this context). و‌گر introduces an alternative conditional 'or if', followed by دادخواه 'seeker of justice' (one who petitions for justice). The whole segment lists the two possible kinds of visitors to the court.
segments.s546-g77: بدندی is an archaic/poetic plural verb from a root meaning 'to bare the teeth / to glare', here best rendered 'they glowered' or 'they showed hostility'. به + سر استواران اوی is a prepositional phrase 'at the heads/chiefs of his stalwart men' where سر = 'chief/head' and استواران = 'the steadfast/loyal ones'. اوی marks possession 'his'.
segments.s546-g78: بپرسیدن is the infinitive/verbal form 'to ask' but here functions as the coordinated action 'and asked' (purpose or coordinated verb). از introduces the object/source 'about/from', followed by کارداران اوی 'his managers/officials' (کارداران = stewards/managers, اوی = 'his'). The clause therefore means they questioned his officials.
segments.s546-g79: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'who' / 'that'. دادست here is an adjectival/predicative or archaic verbal form meaning 'is/was just toward' or 'gave justice' (i.e. dealt out justice). ازیشان = 'from them' or 'toward them' (literally 'of/from them'). و بگرفت چیز = 'and he took/seized things' (بگرفت = 'he seized/took', چیز = 'things/possessions'). The clause says what was done to/for those people.
segments.s546-g80: وزیشان is an archaic compound meaning 'from them' (وز as a prepositional element + ایشان 'them'); که introduces a further relative 'who'; خسپد repeats the earlier poetic verb 'becomes pale/turns white' used of someone stricken by worry, and به تیمار 'with care/anxiety' gives the cause; نیز 'also/likewise' adds that the same applied to such anxious ones. The phrase ties back to the previous clause (he seized things also from those who were pale with care).
segments.s546-g233: نماندی is past tense with the negative prefix ن- plus the 2nd-person singular ending -دی: 'you did not remain/stay'. که introduces the subordinate/relative clause 'so that/that', and بودی is the past of بودن ('you were'). کسی را places 'someone/anyone' as a definite/affected object with را. نیاز here means 'need' (i.e. being in need or the one who needs). The clause literally reads 'you did not remain that you were anyone's need' — idiomatically: 'you did not stay to be anyone's dependence.'
segments.s546-g234: نگه داشتی is a light verb construction: نگه (keeping) + داشتی (past of داشتن, 'you had/kept') = 'you kept/retained'. سختی خویش راز strings together three nouns/possessive elements: سختی 'hardship', خویش 'one's own/nature' and راز 'secret'; the enclitic ش (here written as separate ش) marks 'his/her/its' (possessive) attached to خوی in the sequence. Together: 'you kept the hardship of your disposition secret.'
segments.s546-g235: به میدان شدی places the destination/venue before the verb: به میدان 'to the field/arena' + شدی (past of شدن, here 'you went/arrived/appeared'). بامداد پگاه are adverbials of time ('at dawn/early morning') placed after the verb for poetic cadence; both intensify the time of departure. The whole equals 'you went out to the field at early dawn.'
segments.s546-g236: برفتی is a past-tense verb 'you departed/went off'. The following nominal fragment 'کسی کو بدی دادخواه' is elliptical (typical in poetry): کسی 'someone/anyone' + کو used here as an older/poetic relative/connector (introducing 'who/that') + بدی 'wrong/evil' + دادخواه 'seeker of justice/complainant'. Read together: 'anyone who had been wronged came seeking justice.' The verb linking the second idea is omitted for concision but understood.
segments.s546-g237: This clause is compressed and poetic: نچستی here functions as a negative/characterization (you were not of the sort to...) followed by بداد 'injustice/oppression' and اندر 'in/within'. آزرم is a noun here (possibly a poetic name/term for 'realm' or 'honour' in context) and کس 'anyone/person'. The phrase thus means 'you did not commit injustice against anyone within (your domain).'
segments.s546-g238: This is a paired concessive structure using چه... چه meaning 'whether... or...': چه کهتر (whether the lesser) چه فرزند (or the child/descendant). فریادرس is a substantival participle 'rescuer/succour' (one who answers cries for help). Together: 'whether small or great, child or offspring, (you were) their rescuer.' The verb/copula is again omitted but implied.
segments.s546-g239: Another concessive pair: چه کهتر چه مهتر 'whether the lesser or the greater'. به نزدیک اوی places a relational phrase after it: به نزدیک 'to/at the near(ness)' + اوی (old/poetic third-person pronoun 'he/that one') giving 'you (or he) were close to him' — context determines the exact subject, but grammatically this is a prepositional phrase modifying the concessive pair ('whether low or high, (you) were close to him').
segments.s546-g240: نجستی here is a verbal form from the root جستن (in older poetry the form can mean 'did not hasten/seek' or 'did not seize on/embrace'); combined with همی (an emphatic/continuous particle 'ever/indeed') it yields a negative characterization: 'you did not (ever) take up/seek'. رای تاریک اوی is a noun phrase 'his dark counsel/plan' (رای = counsel/plan, تاریک = dark/obscure, اوی = his). So the clause means 'you never embraced/followed his dark counsel.'
segments.s546-g129: This clause is a past-tense reporting clause + quoted command: گفتی (you said) is 2nd-person past and takes an indirect command introduced by که. به سالار is a prepositional object 'to the commander' (به = 'to/for'). سستی مکن is a negative imperative: سستی = 'laxity/weakness' and مکن = 'do not' — literally 'do not be lax.'
segments.s546-g130: Elliptical imperative clause giving two injunctions. همان modifies تیز (here meaning 'be just/so — swift/keen') and links by و to the second negative imperative پیش‌دستی مکن ('do not take the initiative' / 'do not preempt'). مکن is the negative imperative applied to پیش‌دستی (the act of preemption/initiative).
segments.s546-g131: A descriptive verbless clause typical of Persian narrative: همیشه 'always' + به پیش 'in front/forward' locates the following noun. سپه‌دار is a compound (سپه + -دار) meaning 'commander' and is followed by پیل ('elephant'), i.e., 'the commanders of the elephants' or 'elephant-commanders' who are placed at the front.
segments.s546-g132: This is a noun plus adjective plus prepositional phrase: طلایه 'vanguard' modified by پراگنده 'scattered,' followed by بر چار میل expressing extent/distance ('over/for four miles') with بر as the preposition and چار میل giving the distance.
segments.s546-g133: نخستین 'first' qualifies یکی 'one' (meaning 'one person/unit/element'). گرد لشکر به گرد is a compact, somewhat poetic expression for 'around the army' (گرد = 'about/around', here repeated for emphasis: گرد ... به گرد), so the whole means 'the first one goes about/round the army.'
segments.s546-g134: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when', پیش آیدت is a verbal phrase 'comes upon you' (آید + enclitic ت indicating 'to you'), and روز ننگ و نبرد is the noun phrase 'the day of shame and battle' serving as the subject/time that 'comes upon you.' The clause as a whole = 'when the day of shame and battle befalls you.'
segments.s546-g135: An imperative instruction: به لشکر چنین گوی = 'say this to the army' (به = 'to', لشکر = 'army', چنین = 'thus/so', گوی here is an imperative 'say'). کاین خود کیند is a subordinate clause introduced by کاین ('that this/that which'): خود emphasizes 'themselves' and کیند is an archaic/poetic 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive 'they do/let them do' expressing the commanded action.
segments.s546-g136: بدین here functions as 'in/with this' (classical compound often written بدین), followed by رزمگاه 'battlefield' and اندرون 'inside/within'; برچیند is a 3rd-person form meaning 'to clear away / to set/arrange' (poetic). Together the phrase reads 'let them (or they) clear/occupy the interior of this battlefield.'
segments.s546-g185: بدان تا is a temporal/connecting phrase: بدان (here built from ب‑ + دان in an older/poetic form) introduces the circumstance, followed by تا 'until/when'. پذیره شدندی سپاه is the clause’s verb + subject: پذیره 'reception/acceptance' + شدندی, an archaic past/plural form of شدن ('were made' / 'became'), and سپاه 'the army' as the affected group; word order places the verbal idea (acceptance) before the agent (سپاه).
segments.s546-g186: This short clause names what was done next: بیاراستی is an older/adverbial or verbal form related to آراستن (to adorn/array) used here in a poetic past sense, followed by the noun phrase تخت پیروز شاه ('the victorious king’s throne'), where تخت ('throne') is head noun modified by پیروز ('victorious') and then the possessor شاه ('king').
segments.s546-g187: کشیدی is a past/durative verb form (poetic past of کشیدن, 'drew/formed') governing the following noun phrase. The phrase پرستنده هر سو رده is a compact nominal group: پرستنده 'worshippers/devotees' is the subject, هر سو 'on every side/toward every direction' is an adverbial phrase, and رده 'rank/row' indicates the formation they took; Persian permits this order (verb + subject phrase) in poetry.
segments.s546-g188: همه جامه‌هاشان is a noun phrase meaning 'all their garments' with همه preceding the noun and the possessive suffix‑شان attached to جامه‌ها. به زر is a prepositional phrase ('in/with gold') where به functions like 'with/in', and آژده here is an attributive adjective in poetic diction describing how the garments appeared.
segments.s546-g189: فرستاده را is the definite direct object marked by را. پیش خود here means 'in his presence' or 'before him' (پیش = 'in the presence of', خود = 'himself'). خواندی is an archaic past form of خواندن used in the sense 'to call/summon' (not 'read') — so grammatically this is a simple past transitive clause: 'he summoned the messenger before him.'
segments.s546-g190: به نزدیکی تخت is a locative phrase: به + نزدیکی ('to/at the nearness of') + تخت ('throne'), expressing location 'near the throne'. بنشاندی is an archaic past causative/active form from نشاندن ('to seat, to place'), meaning 'he caused (him) to sit' / 'he seated (him)'.
segments.s546-g191: به پرسش گرفتی is an idiomatic verbal phrase: به پرسش 'into question' + گرفتی (past of گرفتن, 'you/ he took') together mean 'you questioned' or 'he put (him) to questions'. همه راز اوی is a noun phrase 'all his secrets' with همه preceding راز and the possessive pronoun اوی after the noun (poetic form of his/her).
segments.s546-g192: This is a list introduced by ز (poetic 'about/from/concerning'): ز نیک و بد و نام و آواز اوی enumerates the topics asked about — نیک و بد ('good and bad'), نام ('name' or 'reputation'), and آواز (here used in the sense of renown/what one is 'spoken of' for) — with اوی again the possessive 'his'. The conjunction و links the items in the customary Persian coordination order.
segments.s546-g137: از ایشان is a prepositional phrase 'from them' introducing the contrasted quantity. صد اسپ افگن is a noun phrase 'a hundred (bright/ardent) steeds' where افگن is a postpositive descriptive element attached to اسپ. از ما یکی is a short contrastive verbless clause 'from us, one' (the copula/verb is omitted, as is common in poetry) emphasizing the disparity.
segments.s546-g138: همان picks out 'that same' hundred just mentioned. به پیش is an adverbial phrase 'to/into the front; ahead' (literally 'towards the front'). یکی اندکی = 'one, a little' expresses that a single man/steed should be sent a little ahead; the verb ('send' or 'go') is omitted and understood from context.
segments.s546-g139: شما را is the marked indirect object 'for you' (را here emphasizes/respects the addressees). همه + پاک + برنا و پیر form a coordinated description of the addressees: 'all of you, pure/clear, young and old' (پاک and برنا / پیر are adjectival modifiers placed after همه in poetic order).
segments.s546-g140: ستانم is first‑person (I) present/intentional 'I will obtain / I will take (for you)'; it governs the object that follows. همه خلعت از اردشیر is the object phrase 'all the robes (of honour) from Ardashir' — خلعت is the direct object and از اردشیر specifies the source. Word order places the verb before its object, as is common in classical Persian poetry.
segments.s546-g141: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. اسپ افگند uses افگند (past/causative 'sent/let loose' or 'threw forth') with اسپ to mean 'sent forth horses' (a cavalry deployment); لشکر from هر دو روی locates that action 'from both sides/flanks'. The clause sets the temporal/background condition for the following warning.
segments.s546-g142: نباید که is an impersonal negative obligation 'it must not be that...' or 'one should not allow that...'. The following nominal phrase گردان پرخاشجوی ('an aggressive troop') functions as the disallowed state/predicate; no finite verb follows because the prohibition is expressed by نباید که + nominal predicate.
segments.s546-g143: بیاید که is an impersonal modal construction 'it may happen that / it is possible that'. ماند is the verbal predicate 'remains'; تهی modifies the following noun and means 'empty'. قلب گاه is a poetic genitival/compound order ('the place/seat of the heart' i.e. the centre), inverted for metrical reasons, so the clause says that the central place may be left empty.
segments.s546-g144: وگر is the conditional 'and if / but if'. چند بسیار functions idiomatically 'however many / though numerous' (چند = however many, بسیار = many). باشد سپاه places the verb باشد 'be/may be' before the subject سپاه (inverted order typical of poetic diction), yielding 'even if the army be very numerous.'
segments.s546-g9: سواری (a noun) + بیاموزد (3rd-person singular verb) is a verb phrase meaning 'learns horsemanship/ riding.' In Persian the verb follows its object(s); here سواری is the direct object. و links the next noun phrase رسم جنگ ('the manner/practice of war'), where رسم (manner/practice) modifies جنگ (war) in a noun–noun construction.
segments.s546-g10: The preposition به marks instrument/means ('with/by') for the following weapon nouns. The list is coordinated with و 'and'; the second به repeats before تیر to parallel the first. Each noun is in its basic (bare) form and functions as an instrumental noun.
segments.s546-g11: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when.' The noun کودک is the topic/subject ('the child'). ز used poetically marks source/reason 'by/through' (ز کوشش = 'through effort'). به + مردی شدی is the predicate: به مردی (into manhood) + شدی (2nd-person singular past 'you became'), so grammatically it says 'when the child, through effort, you became a man' (classical Persian often omits an explicit subject).
segments.s546-g12: بهر marks purpose/occasion ('for/as to/for the sake of'). بخششی here is a noun meaning 'a gift/charitable giving.' در in this line functions as 'at / in the place of' (here best read as 'at the gate/door'); بی is the (poetic) preposition/particle attaching to آهو or part of a phrase, and آهو is 'gazelle.' بدی is used here as a past action 'you gave/presented' (an archaic verbal form), so the clause says 'you gave a gazelle at the gate as a gift.'
segments.s546-g13: ز (poetic form of از) marks origin 'from'; کشور is 'country/realm.' به marks destination 'to'; درگاه شاه is a prepositional phrase 'the king's court/presence.' آمدند is 3rd-person plural past 'they came.' The word order is source → destination → verb (common in poetry).
segments.s546-g14: بدان is a fused prepositional phrase (ب + آن) meaning 'to that.' نامور is an adjective 'renowned' that modifies بارگاه 'palace/court.' آمدند repeats the verb 'they came,' so the line emphasizes arrival at that famous palace. Word order is preposition + adjective + noun + verb.
segments.s546-g15: نوشتی is a past tense verb 'they wrote/inscribed.' عرض here functions as a verbal noun/infinitival sense 'to present/enter' (in the sense of making an entry or presenting a name), so the phrase reads 'they wrote/entered the name in the diwan.' نام is 'name' and دیوان here is best read as the register/record (the official roll). اوی is the possessive 'his.'
segments.s546-g16: This clause describes furnishing/adornment of the royal residence: کاخ 'palace' and ایوان 'iwan/porch' are coordinated by و and modified by the possessive اوی 'his.' بیاراستی is a poetic/archaic verbal form from آراستن meaning 'adorned/furnished' or an intensive of arrangement; here it serves as the verb governing کاخ و ایوان اوی.
segments.s546-g105: وگر is a conditional particle 'if'. تاب is a noun meaning 'strength/endurance' (here 'any remaining strength'); بودی is the past-form/existential 'there was' used in the conditional clause. به سرش اندرون is a locative phrase 'within his head' where سر 'head' takes the enclitic possessive -ش 'his' and اندرون 'inside' follows to mark the internal location.
segments.s546-g106: به دل کین = 'in the heart, (there was) hatred' — به is the locative/prepositional marker and دل is 'heart' used as the seat of feeling; کین is 'hatred/vengeance'. و اندر جگر جوش خون links another locative: اندر = 'in/within', جگر is the visceral seat of passion, and جوش خون is a compound 'boiling/ surging of blood' (a metaphor for fierce anger). Word order is noun phrases joined by و (= and).
segments.s546-g107: سپه را is the direct object 'the army' marked by the object particle را. بدادی is the verb 'you gave' (past); the edition's lemma list is imperfect here but syntactically it's the past-tense 2nd/3rd-person form meaning 'gave'. سراسر درم is an adverbial phrase 'throughout/all (my) dirhams' — درم = dirham(s), i.e. money, and سراسر 'entire/throughout' quantifies it.
segments.s546-g108: بدان here functions as a connective/adverbial 'by that / thereby / with that' (classical بِدان), introducing purpose with تا 'so that/in order that'. نباشند is the negative subjunctive/optative 'may not be / there be not' (3rd plural), followed by یک تن 'not a single person' (تن = person) and دژم 'sullen/angry'. The whole clause gives the purpose/result of the giving.
segments.s546-g109: یکی before a noun is an indefinite determiner 'a certain' or 'one'. پهلوان 'champion/hero' is the noun sought; خواستی is the past 'you wanted/sought'. نامجوی is a compound adjective/noun 'name-seeking' (one who seeks renown) and follows the noun it modifies, as is common in Persian.
segments.s546-g110: This segment is a string of epithets modifying that champion: خردمند 'wise', و 'and', بیدار 'awake/alert', و 'and', آرامجوی 'seeker of peace/rest' (آرام + جوی). There is no copula; these adjectives follow the noun and enumerate his qualities.
segments.s546-g111: دبیری is a noun meaning the office/skill of a secretary (secretariat). به آیین = 'in ritual/according to custom' (به as an adverbial 'in the manner of'), و با دستگاه = 'and with apparatus/system' (با = 'with', دستگاه = 'system; court/establishment'), together describing the secretary's method or qualifications.
segments.s546-g112: که introduces a relative clause 'who ...'. دارد + نگاه is a light-verb construction 'has/keeps a watch/guard'. ز is the poetic/prepositional 'from/against' and links بیداد لشکر 'the army's injustice/oppression' as the object of that watching: i.e. he watches/guards so as to prevent or oppose injustice affecting the army. Word order is typical classical Persian: relative clause follows and modifies the preceding noun phrase.
segments.s546-g121: به چیزِ کسان is a prepositional phrase: به + چیز + کسان = 'about/in the things of people' (literally 'to the things of people'). کس میازید is a present-tense verb phrase (میازید = 'takes pride, boasts'); word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect so the verb follows the object phrase. دست is fronted here as a noun in apposition or as a topic ('the hand/power'), left without an explicit possessive marker because Persian poetry allows such compact expressions.
segments.s546-g122: هرانکس که او هست یزدان‌پرست is a relative/nominal clause: هرانکس که = 'whoever (that)'; او هست is a simple copular clause 'he is'; یزدان‌پرست is a compound adjective/noun meaning 'God‑worshipper, pious person'. The clause identifies whose 'hand' is meant in the previous segment.
segments.s546-g123: به دشمن هرانکس که بنمود پشت is a subordinate clause: به دشمن = 'toward the enemy / on the enemy' (the preposition + object); هرانکس که = 'whoever (that)'; بنمود + پشت = a verbal phrase meaning 'showed/turned (his) back' — بنمود is the past stem used poetically for 'show/turn', and پشت is the direct object 'back'.
segments.s546-g124: شود زان سپس روزگارش درشت is the main result clause: شود is the present/subjunctive/impersonal form of 'become' ('becomes'); زان سپس = 'thereupon/after that' (زان = 'from that', سپس = 'then'); روزگارش = 'his time/fate/fortune' (possessive -ش); درشت = 'rough/harsh' describing the fate. Word order places the verb first for emphasis (poetic inversion).
segments.s546-g125: اگر دخمه باشد به چنگال اوی is a conditional protasis: اگر = 'if'; دخمه = 'a dungeon/sepulchre/gloomy cell' (here idiomatically 'a dark cell/sepulchre'); باشد is the present subjunctive/auxiliary 'is/there is' used after اگر to mark the condition; به چنگال اوی = 'in/at his talon' (به + چنگال + اوی = 'in his claw'). The order is typical poetic positioning of locative phrase after the verb.
segments.s546-g126: وگر بند ساید بر و یال اوی continues the alternative condition: وگر = 'but if / or if'; بند here means 'fetter/bond'; ساید is an archaic/poetic verbal/adjectival form meaning 'is put/laid' or 'established' (poetic diction); بر = 'on/upon'; و یال اوی = 'and his neck/mane' (یال = 'mane/crest' with his possessive اوی). The construction lists two possible bad conditions bound to the same subject.
segments.s546-g127: ز دیوان دگر نام او کرده پاک uses ز = 'from' (poetic), دیوان دگر = 'another register/record' or 'another official roll'; نام او کرده پاک is a resultive verblike phrase: نام او = 'his name/reputation' (نام here = 'reputation'); کرده (perfect participle of کردن) = 'made'; پاک = 'clean/pure' — together 'his name has been made clean' (i.e., removed/erased or purified) with the passive/result sense common in classical Persian.
segments.s546-g128: خورش خاک و رفتنش بر تیره خاک is a pair of short nominal clauses describing his fate: خورش خاک = 'his food is dust' (خورش = 'food/dish' used to say one eats dust, i.e., reduced to dust); و رفتنش بر تیره خاک = 'and his going (رفتنش, here meaning his passing/death) is onto dark earth' (بر = 'onto/upon', تیره خاک = 'dark earth'). رفتنش is the noun/gerund form 'his going', used idiomatically for death.
segments.s546-g145: چنان کن is an imperative phrase 'do so' (چنان + کن). که introduces a subordinate/purpose clause 'so that / that'. با + nouns marks accompaniment; here با میمنه میسره lists the two wings ('with the right (میمنه) and the left (میسره)') in compressed poetic order without a conjunction.
segments.s546-g146: بکوشند is a third-person plural present/jussive form (بـ- prefix + کوشش stem) used like a hortative/jussive 'let them strive'. The subject جنگ‌آوران follows the verb (typical in poetry), and یکسره is an adverb 'entirely/altogether' modifying the verb.
segments.s546-g147: همان نیز 'the same also' = 'do likewise'. با introduces accompaniment; the phrase با میسره میمنه again names the two wings (here in reversed order). The nouns are juxtaposed for rhythmic/poetic effect rather than linked by a conjunction.
segments.s546-g148: بکوشند repeats the hortative 'let them strive'. و links the next imperative/elliptical clause; دلها همه 'all hearts' is the direct object/topic, and بر + بنه 'upon the heap/put on the stake' is a prepositional phrase—poetically elliptical for 'place/commit all hearts to it' (بنه = small heap/heap).
segments.s546-g149: بود here functions as a jussive/optative 'let it be / remain' (poetic instruction). لشکر قلب is an attributive construct 'army of the heart'; بر جای خویش is a prepositional phrase 'on its place' with خویش expressed as خوی + ش (poetic segmentation) meaning 'its own'.
segments.s546-g150: کس از قلبگه نگسلد is a negative jussive: کس = 'no one', از = 'from', قلبگه 'the heart-seat', نگسلد (neg. prefix نـ + گسلد) = 'should not loosen/break' (third-person subjunctive/jussive). پای خویش 'his foot/footing' (پای + خویش) expresses 'his post/position'—i.e. 'let no one abandon his post.'
segments.s546-g151: وگر is a conditional 'if / but if'. قلب ایشان 'their heart' places the pronoun after the noun (construct order). بجنبد is a present/subjunctive/intransitive verb 'stir / move', and ز جای = 'from its place' (ز is the poetic form of از).
segments.s546-g152: تو is the vocative/imperative 'you'; با لشکر 'with the army' marks accompaniment. از قلب‌گاه 'from the heart-seat' indicates the point of departure, اندر + آی is an emphatic/poetic imperative 'enter/come in' (اندر 'into' + آی 'come!').
segments.s546-g209: همان acts as a continuative/demonstrative ('that/thus') pointing back to the previous action; تا is the temporal conjunction 'until'; فراوان is the predicative adjective 'numerous/abundant' and شود is the intransitive verb 'become' (present/past-subjunctive sense in classical style), so the clause literally reads 'thus until (they) became numerous'; زیردست is the noun 'subordinates' that is the subject which becomes numerous.
segments.s546-g210: خورش ساخت is a light causative/past construction 'made/prepared a meal' (ساخت = 'made'); با is the preposition 'with/using'; جایگاه here means a place/seat/status; نشست is past 'sat' (he took his seat). The clause describes preparing a feast or meal and then sitting in his place—word order is typical poetic (verb after object).
segments.s546-g211: ازو is the genitive 'of him' (meaning 'his'); نام نیکی is a compound meaning 'good name' i.e. 'good reputation'; بود is the past copula 'was'; در جهان is the locative phrase 'in the world' — overall 'his good name was in the world' = 'he had a good reputation publicly.'
segments.s546-g212: The pair چه ... و چه ... functions correlative here as 'both ... and' / 'whether ... or'; بر آشکار = 'in public/openly' (بر + آشکار used adverbially) and اندر نهان = 'inwardly/secretly' using literary اندر + نهان. This gives 'both openly and in secret.'
segments.s546-g213: چو here means 'like/as' (classical use: 'such as'), so چو او modifies شهریاری نبود to mean 'there was no sovereignty/ruler like him.' در جهان is the locative 'in the world'; نبود is the negative past copula 'was not' applied to شهریاری ('sovereignty/rulership').
segments.s546-g214: پس از is the temporal prepositional phrase 'after'; مرگ او = 'his death'; یادگاری is 'memorial/inscription/memento' and نبود is the negative past 'was not' — literally 'after his death there was no memorial (of him)'.
segments.s546-g215: منم is the emphatic copula 'I am'; ویژه is 'special/dedicated' modifying the following role; زنده‑کن is best read as an agentive compound ('one who makes alive' or 'reviver') where کن functions as an agentive/formative element rather than an imperative; نام اوی = 'his name' (اوی = his). Together: 'I am specially the reviver of his name.'
segments.s546-g216: مبادا is a negative optative 'may it not be / let it not happen'; جز از = 'except by/of'; نیکی انجام اوی = 'the goodness of his deeds' (انجام here = 'result/outcome/deed'). The clause expresses the wish that nothing about his actions be anything but good.
segments.s546-g25: شهنشاه را نامه کردی بران is a compact past-tense clause: شهنشاه را (Shah + object marker را) places the addressee of the letter before the verb for emphasis; نامه کردی is a periphrastic past (نامه + کردی) meaning 'you wrote/sent a letter'; بران (بر آن in fused poetic form) attaches as an adverbial/locative 'about/on that' — here 'concerning them'. Word order (object + noun + past verb) is typical for Ferdowsi's narrative diction.
segments.s546-g26: هم ... هم ... is a correlative pair meaning 'both ... and ...'. از and ز are synonymous prepositions ('from/of'), the second is the more poetic/z-variant; they introduce the two groups being reported about. بی‌هنر is a descriptive plural group 'the unskilled', and جنگ‌آوران is the plural 'warriors'. The sequence lists the two categories the letter concerned.
segments.s546-g27: چون functions here as a temporal conjunction ('as/when'); جهاندار is the subject 'the sovereign'; نامه is the object 'the letter'; برخواندی is the past-tense verb meaning 'read aloud' (in this context, the sovereign caused the letter to be read or read it aloud himself). The clause is a temporal subordinate clause introducing what happened next.
segments.s546-g28: فرستاده را is the definite object-marked noun 'the messenger'. پیش here means 'in the presence of / before (him)'. بنشاندی is the past (2nd/archaic narrative) form of 'to seat/place' meaning 'had (him) seated' or 'caused to sit'. Together the clause says the messenger was seated before the sovereign.
segments.s546-g29: هنرمند را marks 'the skillful man' as the definite object (را). خلعت is the gift 'robe of honour' and آراستی is the past-tense verb 'you adorned / you arrayed' (آراستن). The phrase means 'you bestowed a robe of honour on the skilled man'.
segments.s546-g30: ز گنج introduces a source 'from the treasury/treasure'. آنچ is a relative/indefinite 'that which / whatever', modified by the comparative adjective پرمایه‌تر 'more substantial/richer'. خواستی is the past 2nd-person 'you wished/you asked (for)'. The clause says he sought from the treasure whatever was the richer/more substantial.
segments.s546-g31: چو is the temporal 'when'; کردی is the past 2nd-person 'you did'; نگاه اندران is a verbal-nominal phrase 'cast a glance on/at them' (نگاه + اندرَان 'among/at them'), and بی‌هنر qualifies those being looked at 'the unskilled'. The whole segment sets the condition 'when you looked at the unskilled among them'.
segments.s546-g32: نبستی is the negative past of بستن ('you did not bind/you did not place/you did not send'), here idiomatically 'you did not put/assign'. میان جنگ را more literally 'into the middle of the battle' (میان = middle/in the midst), with را marking جنگ as definite; بیشتر 'more' modifies the verb phrase. Together: 'you did not put more of them into the midst of the fight.'
segments.s546-g169: ازین پندها = از + این (arch. spelling 'ین') + پندها: literally 'from these counsels/admonitions.' هیچ‌گونه is an adverbial modifier 'in no way / not at all.' مگرد here is a classical/poetic negative particle (imperative/sentential) meaning 'do not turn aside / do not depart' (it forbids leaving the counsels) and connects to the following conditional clause.
segments.s546-g170: چو خواهی که مانی تو بی‌رنج و درد is a conditional clause: چو + خواهی 'if you wish' (present-tense desiderative), که introduces the subordinate content 'that you remain.' مانی is the verb 'remain/stay' in a subjunctive/optative sense ('that you may remain'), followed by the emphatic pronoun تو. بی‌رنج و درد is a coordinated predicate 'free from suffering and pain' describing the desired state.
segments.s546-g171: به پیروزی اندر = به (for/toward) + پیروزی (victory) + اندر (in/for): a purposive phrase 'for the sake of victory.' به یزدان گرای is an exhortative/imperative: 'turn (thyself) to God' — گرای is the verb 'incline/turn' (classical form) governing یزدان as the object/direction.
segments.s546-g172: که introduces the result/subordinate clause 'that ...': او is the third‑person subject 'he (God).' باشدت is an optative/subjunctive form ('may he be to you' / 'let him be for you'), where -ت marks the dative/benefactive 'to you.' بی‌گمان is a sentential adverb 'undoubtedly,' and رهنمای is a predicate noun 'guide.'
segments.s546-g173: ز جایی که آمد فرستاده‌ای = ز (from) + جایی (a place/where) + که (relative 'that/where') + آمد (came): 'from a place whence came ...'. فرستاده‌ای is the 'sent one' (a messenger) derived from فرستادن; here it functions as a noun 'a messenger/one sent.'
segments.s546-g174: ز ترکی و رومی و آزاده‌ای = a simple coordination introduced by ز (from/of): 'from a Turk and a Roman and a noble/free man.' Each ethnic/character term is a noun here (not merely adjectival).
segments.s546-g175: ازو مرزبان آگهی داشتی = ازو ('from/of him') + مرزبان (the frontier-commander) + آگهی (information/knowledge) + داشتی (you had). Word order is topical: the source 'ازو مرزبان' precedes the fact 'آگهی داشتی' — 'you were informed by that marzban' or 'you had knowledge from him.'
segments.s546-g176: چنین کارها خوار نگذاشتی = چنین کارها 'such deeds/things' + خوار 'despised/low' + نگذاشتی 'you did not allow/let.' نگذاشتی is the negative past of گذاشتن used here as 'did not permit/let (them be).' The whole clause means 'you did not let such deeds be despised.'
segments.s546-g201: کسی کردنش را is a causative/appointing construction: کسی ('someone') + کردن ('to make/appoint') with the clitic ش marking the person made (کردنش = 'making him') and را as the definite/object marker. فرستاده‌وار is an adjectival/adverbial phrase formed with -وار meaning '‑like' and qualifies the act: 'in a messenger‑like manner' or 'as an envoy.' Word order places the description before the verb that follows.
segments.s546-g202: بیاراستی is an archaic/poetic past 2nd‑person singular of بی‌آراستن ('to array/ornament'), here functioning as 'you arrayed/adorned' and taking خلعت شهریار as its direct object. خلعت شهریار ('the king's robe/robe of honour') is the object being bestowed; شهریار is the possessive noun modifying خلعت. The verb governs the noun phrase directly.
segments.s546-g203: به هر سو is a prepositional phrase 'to every direction/quarter' placed before the verb; فرستاد is the simple past 'he sent'; پس is a sentence adverb meaning 'then' or 'after that' and follows the verb in this poetic order; موبدان (mōbedān) is the direct object 'the priests/mobeds' who are being sent.
segments.s546-g204: بی‌آزار و بیداردل are coordinate adjectives describing the mobeds: بی‌آزار 'harmless/gentle' and بیداردل 'wakeful‑hearted/tender‑hearted'. بخردان is a dative/benefactive phrase formed with the prefix ب‑ plus خردان (the wise), meaning 'to/for the wise' or 'toward wise men' and indicates the intended beneficiaries/recipients of the mobeds' mission.
segments.s546-g205: که تا here introduces a purpose/result clause: که + تا = 'so that/so as to.' هر سوی شهرها is a locative phrase 'every side/quarter of the cities' (literally 'every direction, the cities'), and ساختند is the past plural verb 'they built.' The verb comes at the end of the clause, as is normal in Persian.
segments.s546-g206: بدین is a fixed prepositional compound meaning 'with/for this' (ب‑ + این in older usage) and introduces the thing for which something is done; نیز 'also' adds 'likewise'; گنجی is the indefinite accusative 'a treasure'; بپرداختند is the past plural 'they paid' or 'they bestowed.' Together: 'they also paid a treasure for this.'
segments.s546-g207: بدان is the 2nd‑person singular imperative of دانستن ('know!'); تا introduces the subordinate content clause 'that/...'; کسی را که بی‌خانه بود is a relativized object phrase 'anyone who was houseless' with را marking the (definite/particular) person and بود as the past copula at clause end ('was'). The sequence knowingly introduces a general statement about such people.
segments.s546-g208: نبودش is the negative past copula with the clitic ش ('it/for him was not' = 'he had no…'); نوا 'melody/song' is the noun negated ('he had no song'); بخت 'fortune/destiny' followed by بیگانه 'alien/strange' forms a second clause 'his fortune was alien' with بود as the copula. The two parts are coordinated observations about the houseless person's lack and ill fate.
segments.s546-g241: ز دادش is a causal phrase: ز = 'by/because of' followed by دادش ('his justice', داد + enclitic -ش). جهان یکسر آباد کرد is a verb phrase with جهان (the direct object 'the world') + یکسر (adverb 'entirely') + آباد کرد ('made prosperous'); کرد is the simple past 'made/caused'. The whole segment means 'by his justice he made the entire world prosperous.'
segments.s546-g242: دل زیردستان is a genitival phrase 'the hearts of the subjects' (the ezafe is omitted in poetic diction). به خود is a prepositional phrase 'toward/for himself' (خود = 'himself'), followed by شاد کرد ('made [them] glad'); کرد is causative past. The understood subject continues from the previous clause (the ruler), so the sense is 'he made the hearts of his subjects rejoice in him.'
segments.s546-g243: چون گشت is a temporal/causal clause marker: چون here functions like 'when/once' and گشت is the past of گشتن 'became/was made'. جهاندار is the subject 'the sovereign' and با داد جفت is a predicate complement 'paired with justice' (جفت = 'pair, matched'). The clause reads 'when the ruler became matched with justice.'
segments.s546-g244: زمانه is personified 'the times/fortune' as subject. پی او is a locative/pursuit phrase 'after him / following him'. نیارد is here used as the notion 'companion/ally' (poetic usage) and نهفت is the past of نهفتن 'hide/ conceal'; the phrase is a poetic inversion meaning roughly 'fortune did not hide its following after him' — i.e. Fortune openly followed him. The line uses inversion and personification common in epic diction.
segments.s546-g245: فرستاده بودی is a compound past: فرستاده is the past participle 'sent' and بودی (here auxiliary) gives the pluperfect/compound past 'you had sent'. به گرد جهان is a circumstantial phrase 'around the world' (گرد = 'around/about', به گرد جهان = 'to/throughout the world').
segments.s546-g246: A string of coordinated adjectives/nouns describing the agents sent: خردمند 'wise', و 'and', بیدار 'wakeful/alert', کارآگهان (plural of کارآگه) 'those informed/aware about affairs'. In Persian poetic style adjectives and participles often stand in a row and imply 'men' or 'messengers' as the noun being described.
segments.s546-g247: به جایی که بودی is a relative clause 'to a place where there was/was situated' (که introduces the subordinate 'where' clause, بودی = 'was' in the past). زمینی خراب is the noun + adjective 'ruined land' modifying جایی. Together: 'to a place where the land was ruined.'
segments.s546-g248: وگر = 'and if / but if' introduces a conditional; تنگ بودی = 'it/that [place] was narrow' (تنگ = 'narrow/confined', بودی = past 'was'). به رود اندر آب is a locative phrase using two prepositions ('به' + 'اندر') in poetic diction to mean 'into the river's water' or 'to the river into the water' — i.e. a direction/target of action (the verb is elliptically carried over from the prior clause). The line uses compact poetic syntax and some ellipsis of verbs.
segments.s546-g217: فراوان سخن در نهان داشتی is a simple past statement with the past second-person verb داشتی ('you had' / 'you kept'). The adjective فراوان precedes the noun سخن here for emphasis ('much / many words'), در نهان is an adverbial phrase 'in secret / hiddenly' modifying داشتی. The overall order is Subject (implicit you) + object phrase + adverbial + verb, a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s546-g218: به هر جای کارآگهان داشتی: به هر جای ('in/at every place') is an adverbial prepositional phrase; هر جای = 'every place'. کارآگهان is a plural agent noun 'those informed / informants', and داشتی repeats the past second-person 'you had'. The clause means you kept/possessed informants everywhere; the verb agrees with the implied second-person subject.
segments.s546-g219: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. بی‌مایه گشتی is a predicate 'you became penniless' (گشتی = past of گشتن 'became / came to be'); one follows with یکی مایه‌دار where یکی = 'a certain / one' and مایه‌دار = 'wealthy / possessing means'. The sequence gives a contrast: when you were without means, you came upon a wealthy person.
segments.s546-g220: ازان آگهی یافتی شهریار: ازان 'from that / from it' links this clause to the previous; آگهی here = 'information / news' (not 'advertisement'); یافتی is past second-person 'you obtained / you received'. شهریار at the end is given in the vocative or as the addressee ('O king') or the beneficiary of the news. Syntactically this is an object phrase ('from that you gained news') with the vocative appended.
segments.s546-g221: چو بایست برساختی کار اوی: چو 'when', بایست here functions as 'it was necessary' / 'when it was required' followed by the past verb برساختی 'you built / you set up', so the clause means 'when it had to be done, you arranged his affairs'. کار اوی = 'his affair/work' with اوی a possessive pronoun 'his'.
segments.s546-g222: نماندی چنان تیره بازار اوی: نماندی is past negative 'did not remain / did not persist', چنان 'so / such', تیره 'gloomy / ominous', بازار اوی 'his bazaar/market'. The clause states that such a bleak condition no longer remained in his market; word order places the verb first for emphasis (typical in heroic poetry).
segments.s546-g223: زمین برومند و جای نشست is a noun phrase: زمین برومند 'a prosperous / flourishing land' (adjective follows noun, a common classical word order), و 'and', جای نشست 'a place/seat of sitting' where نشست is a verbal noun used to mean 'seat' or 'throne'. The phrase lists two attributes in parallel.
segments.s546-g224: پرستیدن مردم زیردست: پرستیدن is a verbal noun 'worship / adoration' that here links back to جای نشست (a seat for worship); مردم زیردست is a genitival phrase 'the people beneath / the subjects below'. The structure indicates the seat/land is for the people's worship or veneration under him.
segments.s546-g41: وجهِ به + مفعول به‌صورت قیدی: 'به لشکر بیاراست' = 'he arrayed/formed into an army'. فعلِ بیاراست (بیارَسْت) در کاربرد ادبی فعلی ماضی است: 'he adorned/arrayed'. ترتیب کلمات شاعرانه است: مفعولِ کلّی 'گیتی همه' آمده که با فعلِ قبل مرتبط است.
segments.s546-g42: گفتار متصل به جملهٔ قبل: 'شبان گشت' = 'became shepherds'; گشت فعلِ ماضی «شدن/گردیدن» است. حرف ربطِ و بین دو بخش است و فعلِ گشت بر هر دو جزء اطلاق می‌شود (در نتیجه در بند دوم فعل حذف شده و مفهومِ 'شدند' یا 'گشتند' برای 'پرخاش‌جویان' محفوظ است). 'رمه' به صورت مجازی = 'گله/گروه'.
segments.s546-g43: 'به دیوانش' = 'در/در پیشگاه دفتر او'؛ حرف به هنا به‌معنای 'در/در میان' یا 'در پیشِ'. 'کارآگهان' وصفِ کسانِ مطلع/آگاه است (جمع یا صفتِ جمع‌معنایی). فعلِ داشتی شکلِ ماضیِ مخاطب (تو) است: 'you had/kept (them)'.
segments.s546-g44: ساختِ منفی: 'به بی‌دانشی کار نگذاشتی' یعنی 'تو کار را به نادانان نسپردی/نگذاشتی'. 'نگذاشتی' فعلِ ماضیِ منفی به معنی 'اجازه ندادی/نمی‌گذاشتی' است و 'به' نشانِ مفعولِ جهت (کار را به چه کسی/چیز واگذار نکردی).
segments.s546-g45: 'بلاغت نگه داشتندی' = 'تو بلاغت/فصاحت را نگه داشتی/حفظ کردی'. 'نگه' به معنی 'نگهداری' یا 'حراست' و 'داشتندی' شکلِ کهن/شاعریِ گذشته است که معنای 'نگه داشتی' (حفظ کردی) می‌دهد. 'و خط' با و متصل می‌شود و موضوعِ دوم ('خط' = خوش‌نویسی/نسخ) را معرفی می‌کند.
segments.s546-g46: 'کسی کو' ساختی است نسبی/موصولی (در زبان کهن کو گاهی به‌جای که/کهٔ نسبت به کسی به‌کار رفته) که به 'هر کس' اشاره دارد. جملهٔ 'بدی چیره بر یک نقط' به‌معنای 'بدیِ دست/خطِ کسی بر حتی یک نقطه غلبه نکند' است: 'چیره بر' = 'غالب شدن/غلبه کردن بر'. عبارت تلویحاً می‌گوید که حتی یک نقطه (نقص/عیب) نیز به‌جا نماند.
segments.s546-g47: 'چو' = 'آنگاه که/وقتی که'. 'برداشتی' اینجا به معنیِ 'برداشتنِ سخن به‌عنوان رهنمون' یا 'گرفتنِ (آن) کلام' است؛ از نظر فعل، شکلِ گذشتهٔ مخاطب (تو) یا حالت فعلیِ معنایی 'تو گرفتی/برداشتی' است. 'آن سخن رهنمون' = 'آن کلامِ راهنما/راهنمایی'.
segments.s546-g48: 'شهنشاه' فاعلِ جمله است. 'کردیش' در متن کهن گزاره‌ای مرکب است: ریشهٔ 'کرد' (فعلِ انجام دادن/سبب شدن) با ضمیرِ پیوستهٔ اشاره (ایش/ایش = آن/او/آنرا) ترکیب شده؛ یعنی 'او آن را کرد/سبب آن شد'. 'روزی فزون' عبارتِ نتیجه‌ای است: 'روزی' به‌معنای روزی/روزگار/رزق و 'فزون' = 'بیشتر/افزایش یافته'؛ جمع‌بندی: سبب شد روزی(ِ) شاه افزون گردد. (در فهرستِ معانی ارائه‌شده برای برخی واژگان معادلِ دقیقی نیامده، بنابراین برخی موارد نشانه‌گذاریِ معنی با -1 شده‌است.)
segments.s546-g97: This clause has an archaic word order: پیامی (direct object, “a message”) precedes the verb دادی (past 2nd person “you gave/sent”). The two occurrences of به introduce manners/phrases: the first به here functions adverbially ('in / in the manner of') and the second introduces the phrase به آیین ‘with ceremony’ (also adverbial). چرب is an adjective meaning “flattering”; the whole phrase describes the manner in which the message was sent.
segments.s546-g98: بدان here is the fused form of ب (prefix) + دان (know/consider) and functions as an emphatic/imperative-like 'know this' or 'be aware/so that'; تا introduces purpose/result ('so that / in order that'); نباشد is the negative present/subjunctive of بودن meaning 'may not be' or 'there be no'; به before بیداد indicates direction/relation ('in / into / with respect to') and بیداد حرب = 'injustice/ oppression in battle'.
segments.s546-g99: فرستاده is 'the envoy/messenger' (object or topical noun); رفتی is past 2nd-person (you went / you caused to go) and here effectively means 'you sent' or 'you caused the envoy to go.' بر introduces the target/recipient ('to/toward'); دشمنش is 'his enemy' with the enclitic -ش 'his'. The structure is concise and typical of epic/past narration.
segments.s546-g100: که is a subordinating conjunction 'that / whereby / so that' introducing the resulting clause. بشناختی is an archaic/perfect-like form meaning 'recognized / came to know / discovered' (here past action). راز پیراهنش is the definite object 'the secret of his shirt' (راز = 'secret'; پیراهنش = 'his shirt' with enclitic -ش).
segments.s546-g101: شنیدی is past tense 'you heard' (or 'you listened to / you learnt of'); سخن‌گر is a compound noun 'speaker/orator'; خرد qualifies it as 'of wisdom' (a wise speaker). داشتی is past second-person of داشتن and here means 'you had' (i.e. 'you were accompanied by / you possessed'—past possession/state). The sentence lists experiences in paratactic (coordinated) fashion.
segments.s546-g102: This clause lists the sufferings: غم و رنج 'sorrow and hardship' is the noun phrase; بد را (بد as modifier) with را marking the definite object/topic. به before the second بد is adverbial, 'in / with (a) bad [manner]' and داشتی again is past 'you had / you bore.' The repetition of بد creates a terse, proverbial antithesis ('bad to bad' / 'met evil with evil').
segments.s546-g103: بدان here again functions as a fused particle ('thereby/with that' or 'so/thereupon': ب + دان). یافت is the past tense 'he found/obtained'; او is the third-person subject 'he'. خلعت شهریار is the object phrase 'the king's robe' (خلعت = robe of investiture; شهریار = the king). Grammatically, the particle + verb sequence expresses result ('thereupon he obtained...').
segments.s546-g104: همان is a demonstrative/adjective 'the same' or 'that very'; عهد و منشور is a coordinated noun phrase 'covenant and charter' (عهد = covenant/treaty, منشور = charter/inscribed document). با introduces an accompaniment 'with', and گوشوار is an ornament (literally 'earring'); the whole phrase lists the additional items he received along with the robe.
segments.s546-g225: بیاراستی is an emphatic adverb here ('in truth/indeed'). چون functions as a conjunction introducing a temporal/causal clause ('when/since'), and ببایست is a modal past form ('it was necessary' / 'had to be'); کار is the noun 'matter/affair' governed by that clause. Word order is emphatic adverb + conjunction + modal + noun (the thing that needed doing).
segments.s546-g226: نگشتی is the past form of گشتن with a negative sense here ('did not become / did not turn out'); نهانش = نهان + -ش (its hiddenness/secret) is the noun phrase, and به کس آشکار is a prepositional phrase ('clear/known to anyone').
